summary: PR #2619 was a closed, unmerged CM directory split from #2617, scoped by the author to src/melee/cm and listing src/melee/cm/camera.c. The loaded raw diff is empty and decomp-dev reported 'No changes', but a review comment on camera.c shows an attempted replacement of HSD_ASSERTMSG calls in Camera_80029CF8 with raw __assert calls using cm_* data symbols. The reviewer requested changes, stating that edits intended to match data sections are against the contributing guidelines and that the highlighted assert/data-symbol change is a regression even if it improves data percent.

key_files.role: Only file named in PR body and review; review hunk shows Camera_80029CF8 assert handling.

what_changed: The PR body says the slice was split from #2617 by directory/subsystem for easier review and scoped to src/melee/cm.
The PR body lists src/melee/cm/camera.c as the file in scope.
The review hunk shows HSD_ASSERTMSG calls for fov_r and fov_l in Camera_80029CF8 being replaced with explicit if checks that call __assert(cm_803BCBD0, line, cm_803BCC14/cm_803BCC30).
The loaded raw diff is empty and the decomp-dev bot report says 'No changes', so the complete historical patch content is not available in this slice.
smart_moves: The author split a larger source branch by directory/subsystem to make review easier.
The PR body separated preliminary full-branch numbers from authoritative per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting, avoiding overclaiming the slice score.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not replace source-level HSD_ASSERTMSG forms with raw __assert plus data-symbol string references just to improve data-section percentage.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: reviewer says the highlighted change is a regression even if it improves data %
review_comments.md: hunk replaces HSD_ASSERTMSG with __assert(cm_803BCBD0, ..., cm_803BCC14/cm_803BCC30)

decomp_lessons.lesson: For matching slices, data-percent improvements are not sufficient justification for source changes when they regress readable macro-level source forms.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: reviewer cites contributing guidelines and says not to make changes with the intention of matching data sections

naming_conventions.convention: Do not introduce or preserve cm_* address-style data-symbol names for assert filenames/messages when the existing macro form keeps the source string intent clear.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: HSD_ASSERTMSG string literals replaced with cm_803BCBD0, cm_803BCC14, and cm_803BCC30 in a regression hunk

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Avoid using raw assert expansion and data-symbol references as a matching tactic for data sections in ordinary source; keep the source-level HSD_ASSERTMSG when it represents the operation.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: reviewer objects to the raw __assert replacement as a data-section matching regression

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Do not make changes with the intention of matching data sections; the highlighted change is a regression even if it improves data percent.

follow_up_queries: PR 2617 split CM camera.c assert data section
review comments matching data sections regression HSD_ASSERTMSG

evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: PR scope, preliminary report, local checks, listed file
review_comments.md: concrete hunk and reviewer feedback
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot says 'No changes' and review state CHANGES_REQUESTED
notes: The slice has strong evidence for the review lesson because the review hunk and comment are loaded.
The slice has weak evidence for the full patch because changed_files is 0, raw diff is empty, and decomp-dev reported 'No changes'.
Preliminary match percentages in the PR body are explicitly described as full unsplit branch numbers, not authoritative per-PR results.
No merge or acceptance status is inferred; metadata shows closed with merged_at null.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Assert macro regressions for data-section matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: In matching PRs, replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG with raw __assert calls and address-style data symbols can be a review-blocking regression when the motivation is data-section percentage rather than source-level correctness.
evidence_refs: PR #2619 review comment by ribbanya on src/melee/cm/camera.c
Hunk in Camera_80029CF8 replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG with __assert(cm_803BCBD0, ..., cm_803BCC14/cm_803BCC30)

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2619 as an example for assert-report and text-before-data standards
source_update_candidates.text: Possible example: PR #2619 review rejected replacing HSD_ASSERTMSG("fov_r<MTXDegToRad(90.0F)") and HSD_ASSERTMSG("fov_l<MTXDegToRad(90.0F)") with raw __assert calls using cm_* data symbols, because the change targeted data-section matching and was considered a regression.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already cover the rule; this PR provides a concrete review example suitable for curator consideration.

rejection_notes: Do not record the PR body's preliminary full-branch score as a verified result for this PR slice; the PR says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative and the loaded bot report says 'No changes'.
Do not infer that src/melee/cm/camera.c actually changed in the final GitHub diff from this slice alone; metadata reports changed_files 0 and raw diff is empty.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit review comment that data-section-motivated changes are not acceptable and the highlighted change is a regression.
